decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a significant margin (58.6°C vs 85.0°C limit). However, the optimization history indicates diminishing returns from geometry changes and suggests shifting to material tuning. Since the spreader conductivity is already at its upper bound (500.0), the next logical step is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction towards the Dirichlet boundaries.

changes.reason: Doubling the base plate conductivity within the allowed 2x ratio to enhance lateral heat spreading and vertical conduction to the cooled sides, addressing the strategy note to tune materials when geometry stalls.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the base plate
Improved heat flux towards the left and right boundaries
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as the base plate conductivity increases if the bottleneck shifts entirely to the interface or spreader
Further increases may yield negligible thermal improvement relative to computational cost
